claim: siRNA knockdown of A20 accelerates tumor progression in an in vivo murine xenograft model.
evidence_sentences: Inhibiting A20 expression by siRNAs in vitro reduced the growth rates of glioma cells and resulted in G1/S arrest and increased apoptosis.
In a mouse tumor model, local administration of siRNA significantly suppressed solid tumor growth.
CONCLUSIONS A20 was overexpressed both in human glioma tissues and cell lines, and inhibiting A20 expression greatly slowed tumor cell growth in culture and in mice.

conditions: in vivo mouse tumor xenograft model
